ground_truth.title: This Is The Top Car Rental Company In The US, According To The 2025 J.D. Power Study - Travel Noire
ground_truth.main_content: This Is The Top Car Rental Company In The US, According To The 2025 J.D. Power Study Travelers say Enterprise is redefining what a smooth car rental experience looks like in 2025. Photo Credit: Fotografía de eLuVe / Getty Images at airport locations over the past year, awarded Enterprise an impressive score of 734 out of a possible 1,000 points for overall customer satisfaction. , pick-up and drop-off processes, level of trust, vehicle quality, staff interactions, value for price, and effectiveness of digital tools. Enterprise Claims Victory In Competitive Car Rental Market National Car Rental secured the second position with a score of 721, demonstrating strong performance despite being edged out by its corporate sibling. Both Enterprise and National operate under the same parent company, Enterprise Mobility. Advantage Rent A Car tied with Sixt for third place, with both companies scoring 711 points, well above the industry average of 691. Hertz rounded out the top five performers in the study. At the opposite end of the spectrum, Dollar, Fox Rent A Car, and Payless received the lowest satisfaction ratings among the 13 companies evaluated. Study Reveals Benefits Of Counter-Free Rental Experience A key finding from the J.D. Power study showed that customers who bypassed the rental counter experienced higher satisfaction levels and saved nearly eight minutes during the vehicle pick-up process. Customers who went straight to their vehicles reported an average satisfaction score of 704, compared to 662 for those who stopped at the counter first. Despite the clear advantages, approximately 80% of customers still visit the rental counter before picking up their vehicles. The average time to complete rental pick-up was just over 14 minutes for customers who skipped the counter, compared to more than 22 minutes for those who stopped at it. Did You Know? Jamaica is seeing a strong return of international travelers as tourism recovery efforts gain momentum nationwide. Dollar Flight Club's latest report compiled a list of destinations that consistently offer great value for American travelers. From forest lodges to the coastal hideaways infused with peace, these Black-owned retreats are perfect stays for wellness. Autism-friendly hotels and resorts are worth seeking out, especially if traveling with children or loved ones on the spectrum.
